clinical_summary: Standardized protocol for ultrasound-guided central venous catheter (CVC) insertion in the emergency department. Covers internal jugular, subclavian, and femoral approaches with real-time ultrasound guidance as the mandatory standard of care.
contraindications: Absolute: active infection at insertion site, known complete thrombosis of target vein. Relative: coagulopathy (INR > 3.0), platelet count < 50,000, patient unable to be positioned appropriately.
evidence_references:
1. NICE Guidelines CG139 (2022 update)
2. Saugel B, et al. Ultrasound-guided CVC. Crit Care. 2024;28:112.
3. American College of Emergency Physicians Policy Statement on CVC (2023)
